validations.summary: /// Validates the codeset in the context, for example checks that there is only one primary ICD. /// This validation actually deferrs to a validation in core coding, i.e. they are /// universally applicable. ///

/// Validates the codeset against theatre slip. In theory, each CPT code (treatment) /// should have occurred as the result of surgery performed in theatre. However, CPT codes /// can also be used to express medical treatments. So this rule is somewhat questionable. ///
/// Validates whether the coding is confirmed ///
/// Validates the codeset against the care type. Similar to the ValidateCodesetAgainstTheatreSlip, /// the theory is that care types of surgical or maternity should have some form of CPT code. ///
/// Validates that a single impairment code is added ///
/// Validates the private estimate. ///
/// Validates the desired stay exists. ///
/// Validates the total days requested is greater than or equal to actuall days. ///
validations.conditions: context.MedicalEvent.FundingMethodType != FundingMethodTypeCodeData.MedicalAid | context.BillingFilter.StayDurations != null && context.CaseManagementEvent.StayRequested != null | totalDaysRequested < totalActualDays
